description: Raw AI output for the Individual Requirement Quality analysis pass. IDs and stats are NOT present — assigned by post-processing into 04_ai_analyzed.02_resolved.

affected_item.description: A spec item involved in the flag. Use gen_uid as the stable join key — it survives hierarchy changes. Include spec_item_id and gen_hierarchy_number for human readability.

gen_uid.description: Content fingerprint from stage 03. Primary join key — position-independent and change-sensitive.

spec_item_id.description: Human-readable ID (e.g. SWRS-1234) if the requirement carries one. Null for raw specs.

gen_hierarchy_number.description: Positional reference (e.g. 'G3.1-002') for human navigation. Informational only — do not use as a join key.

role.description: primary: the item with the issue. conflicting: what it conflicts with. related: context without being wrong itself.

raw_flag.description: A single flag as emitted by the AI — no id assigned yet.

type.description: FINDING: actionable defect. QUESTION: ambiguity that needs author clarification — raises no severity. OBSERVATION: informational note, no action required.

severity.description: CRITICAL: safety/integrity impact. MAJOR: functional impact. MINOR: quality/clarity. INFO: use for OBSERVATION type only.

description.description: Precise description of the issue. Reference item IDs explicitly.

recommendation.description: Suggested corrective action. Null is acceptable for QUESTION and OBSERVATION types.

reference.description: Normative reference supporting this flag (e.g. 'ISO 26262-6 Table 2, 1b', 'MISRA C:2012 Dir 4.7').

confidence.description: AI self-assessed confidence in the flag. Below 0.6 should be treated as QUESTION regardless of type.

flags.description: All flags generated by the AI for this pass, in document order (by affected primary item).

reviewed_items.description: gen_uid of every spec item you actually reviewed — including those with no flags. Used to detect skipped items.
